rows.description: Computus Francisci Webber Rector in a 2d die November 1766 ad eundem diem revolution 1769.
rows.notes: Top-of-page title is partly faded and written in a long running hand. Year figures (1766, 1769) and the name 'Francis(ci) Webber' are legible but several words (e.g. the Latin phrasing) are abbreviated and uncertain—transcribed to best reading.
Centered section header. The script is fairly clear; spelling is archaic/abbreviated but the intended heading 'Arrearagia Recepta' (Arrearage Receipts) is readable.
Clear alignment with ruling lines. Pounds '3', shillings '12' and pence '8' are unambiguous and within valid ranges. Description 'Morton ad feet annum' legible.
Amounts align clearly: £3 4s 8d. Description is a short dash + 'ad feet Mill' (mill fees); wording slightly abbreviated but unambiguous.
Numbers are very clear: £15 0s 0d. Description 'Yardington Rect pro ann: & dim:' reads clearly as rector's receipt for year and half-year.

rows.description: Jenner: pro grain & 3 by part. an:
rows.notes: Amount reads £32 14s 2½d. The halfpenny symbol (a small loop/ob) is clear; alignment with column lines is consistent. Description contains abbreviations (hand written), interpreted as shown.
Amount reads £15 13s 5½d. Halfpenny symbol visible. 'Kidlington Mills' reading is clear in context of place names.

Amount reads £5 11s 7¾d: pence shows three-farthings symbol ('3q' or '3/4') which is legible. The place/name is somewhat abbreviated; chosen reading 'Garrington Fodler' based on letter shapes but not certain.
Second identical-looking line: £5 11s 7¾d. Fraction three-farthings clearly present. 'Molineux ditto' (ditto to prior) inferred from the script; name reading reasonably clear.
Amount reads £1 14s 0d; digits align well. Description 'Southnewington' is legible; 'Canon' / 'Canon' reading fits the script though could be 'Canon' or similar office name.

Amount reads £4 7s 0½d — the pence shows a halfpenny mark (small loop) after the zero. Place name appears as 'Bensington' (legible); small uncertainty in final letter but context supports this reading.
Amount £9 6s 8d is clear. The place/name is less clear in the handwriting ('Ledene', 'Ledam', or similar); transcribed conservatively as 'Ledene Compy'.
Amount £6 13s 4d is legible and within valid ranges. The name looks like 'Harpforth' or 'Heaforth' in the script; chosen 'Harpforth' as best fit though some letters are ambiguous.
Amount £1 13s 4d clear. Description likely 'Greenhow pro ditto' (pro ditto = as above); some contraction in the last word but name 'Greenhow' readable.
Subtotal line clearly written as £105 2s 8d. Matches the sum of the preceding itemised amounts (validation done). 'E:' marking visible in the margin.
Amount £68 0s 0d clearly written. Description appears to duplicate earlier place name with 'Rect pro an & dim' (rector receipts); wording is slightly abbreviated but legible.

Amount £60 0s 0d is clear. Description reads as a rectory receipt for Kidlington (the last word interpreted as 'Grain' or 'Grin' in the script); reading of final word slightly uncertain but context supports 'Rect'.
Amount reads £0 10s 0d (10 shillings). The place/name is written compactly; transcribed as 'Lidenhall' which matches letter shapes in the entry.
Amount £6 0s 0d clear. Place name is uncertain (reads like 'Gotherwan' or 'Gotherwan'); chosen transcription reflects visible letter forms but is not certain.
Subtotal line under the block with amounts £68, £60, 10s, and £6; the total £154 10s 0d is clearly written and aligns with the column rules.
Centered new-section header; handwriting is somewhat stylised. Read as 'Recepta anni Presentis' (Receipts of the present year); a few letters abbreviated so moderate confidence.

Amount £90 5s 0d is clear. The description is less clear: the first word 'Kidlington' is readable, 'augment' appears as an abbreviation, and the final word is uncertain (read here as 'Castlemens' as closest fit to the handwriting). Lower confidence due to uncertain final word.

Amount £2 15s 0d clear. Description begins with a dash and two surnames/parties; letters ambiguous (could be 'Copes & Brown' or 'Copps & Brewers'). Transcribed as 'Copps & Brewers' with note of ambiguity.

rows.description: South Newington Rect: pro dim. an: ad Sept. annum:
rows.notes: Amount £28 0s 0d is clear. Description appears to be for South Newington rector pro dim (half-year) with a note 'ad Sept. annum' (to September year). Some abbreviation and punctuation cause moderate uncertainty but overall readable.

rows.description: Yardington Rect: pro dim. an: ad Sept. annum:
rows.notes: Amount £5 0s 0d clear. Description parallels the previous line for Yardington; abbreviations (pro dim, ad Sept) are readable in context.
Amount 10s 0d is clear. The place/name looks like 'Lidenhall' or 'Lidenhall' but letters are small and compressed; transcribed conservatively with moderate confidence.
Final total line is clearly written as £126 10s 0d beneath the last group; aligns with column rulings and is unambiguous.
